code_quality.title: Split api.py monolith (5012 lines) into domain-scoped route modules
code_quality.description: api.py has grown to 5012 lines containing 51 route handlers, 119 functions, and all analytics/search/taxonomy/LLM logic. It is the single largest file in the project and the primary source of every maintenance problem in the backend.
code_quality.rationale: A 5012-line file violates every established file-size convention (project CLAUDE.md ceiling: 800 lines). It makes navigation, testing, code review, and incremental refactoring extremely hard. Each domain area (analytics, cases CRUD, search, taxonomy, jobs, LLM council) is independently testable and should live in its own module.
code_quality.currentState: 5012 lines, 51 route handlers, 119 helper functions all in a single Blueprint file. Key domains mixed together: case CRUD (lines 2305-2680), analytics (lines 3339-4186), search (lines 3003-3118), taxonomy (lines 4242-4756), LLM council (lines 4815-4972), jobs/pipeline (lines 3158-3241).
code_quality.proposedChange: Split into domain modules under immi_case_downloader/web/routes/: cases.py (~250 lines), analytics_core.py (~400 lines), analytics_judges.py (~300 lines), analytics_concepts.py (~250 lines), search.py (~200 lines), taxonomy.py (~300 lines), llm_council.py (~200 lines), jobs.py (~100 lines). Shared helpers (_normalise_outcome, _is_win, _split_judges, etc.) go into a new immi_case_downloader/web/analytics_helpers.py module. All blueprints registered in web/__init__.py.
code_quality.codeExample:
# Before: api.py lines 3339-5012 — all analytics in one place
# After:
# routes/analytics/__init__.py  — registers sub-blueprint
# routes/analytics/outcomes.py  — analytics_outcomes()
# routes/analytics/judges.py    — analytics_judges(), analytics_judge_leaderboard(), ...
# routes/analytics/concepts.py  — analytics_legal_concepts(), analytics_concept_effectiveness(), ...
code_quality.bestPractice: Single Responsibility Principle, file cohesion ≤800 lines (project CLAUDE.md)
code_quality.implementation_strategy: Side-effect import pattern: api.py defines api_bp + all shared helpers + constants. Sub-modules import from api.py and register routes onto api_bp. api.py's end imports sub-modules as side effects (noqa: F401, E402). URL structure unchanged. No breaking changes.

plan.description: Create immi_case_downloader/web/routes/api_export.py. Move export_csv() and export_json() into it. Import api_bp, _get_all_cases, _apply_filters, _error from .api. Add `from . import api_export  # noqa: F401, E402` at the end of api.py. Run: python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q to confirm no regressions. Target: 0 test failures.

plan.title: Extract api_pipeline.py (7 routes, including LLM council)
plan.description: Create api_pipeline.py. Move the 7 routes. Note: /pipeline-action has rate limiting decorator — verify @rate_limit is imported correctly from security.py. The llm_council module (llm_council.py) is already a separate file; api_pipeline.py just needs to import LlmCouncil and call its methods. Run: python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q after. Watch for: _job_status dict is module-level in api.py — api_pipeline.py must import it, not redefine it.

plan.title: Extract api_taxonomy.py (7 routes, pure query endpoints)
plan.description: Create api_taxonomy.py. These routes are mostly read-only lookups against VehicleRegistry, LegalConceptsRegistry, and in-memory config. Import visa_registry, legal_concepts_registry from their respective modules. No shared mutable state used. Run tests after. Expected: taxonomy routes are the most self-contained and carry lowest regression risk.

plan.title: Extract api_cases.py (13 routes, case CRUD + search)
plan.description: Create api_cases.py. Import CASE_LIST_COLUMNS, EDITABLE_FIELDS, _get_all_cases, _apply_filters, _error from .api. The batch-delete and delete routes have @rate_limit decorators — import rate_limit from web.security. After extraction, api.py should have only: Blueprint definition, all helper functions, constants, csrf-token route, and the 5 side-effect imports. Run full test suite: python3 -m pytest tests/ -q. Verify all 296 Python unit tests pass.

plan.title: Extract api_analytics.py (20 routes, all analytics + stats)
plan.description: Create api_analytics.py. This is the largest extraction (~2000 lines). Import all shared helpers from .api: _normalise_outcome, _is_win, _round_rate, _split_judges, _split_concepts, _determine_court_type, _win_outcomes_for_court_type, _judge_identity, _collect_cases_for_judge, _judge_profile_payload, _analytics_cache, _analytics_get, _analytics_set, _analytics_cache_key, _get_analytics_cases, _get_all_cases, _apply_filters, _analytics_response, _error, _has_analytics_filters. After this phase, api.py should be ~800 lines. Run full test suite. Key risk: _analytics_cache is module-level — must be the same object referenced by both api.py and api_analytics.py. Verify by checking analytics endpoints still serve cached results between requests.

plan.title: Post-split verification
plan.description: 1. Run python3 -m pytest tests/ -q — all 296 unit tests must pass. 2. Check api.py line count: wc -l immi_case_downloader/web/routes/api.py — target ≤ 900 lines. 3. Start server: python web.py --port 8080 and verify /app/ loads. 4. Spot-check 3 analytics endpoints via curl to confirm cache is shared. 5. Run cd frontend && npm run build to confirm no frontend breakage. 6. Update .aperant status to 'completed'.

code_quality.title: Deduplicate RPC fast-path/fallback pattern repeated in 4+ analytics endpoints
code_quality.description: Every analytics endpoint that supports server-side aggregation (analytics_outcomes, analytics_judges, analytics_legal_concepts, analytics_nature_outcome) contains an identical _rpc_ok = False / try RPC / except Exception: logger.warning / if not _rpc_ok: fallback pattern. This is copy-pasted verbatim 4+ times with only the RPC method name and Counter variable names changing.
code_quality.rationale: DRY principle. Copy-pasted exception handling is the #1 source of silent divergence — when one copy gets a bug fix, the others do not. A generic helper reduces the risk and cuts ~30 lines per endpoint.
code_quality.bestPractice: DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself), error handling at single point

plan.title: Implement _rpc_with_fallback helper in analytics_helpers.py
plan.description: Write a generic helper that accepts an optional RPC callable, an RPC row handler, and a fallback case handler. Handles the try/except/logger.warning pattern centrally.

plan.title: Refactor the 4 analytics endpoints to use the helper
plan.description: Replace the _rpc_ok pattern in analytics_outcomes, analytics_judges, analytics_legal_concepts, and analytics_nature_outcome with calls to _rpc_with_fallback. Run test suite after each change.

code_quality.title: Deduplicate filter_cases() across CsvRepository, SqliteRepository, SupabaseRepository
code_quality.description: All three repository implementations define a filter_cases() method with the same 12-parameter signature (court, year, visa_type, source, tag, nature, keyword, sort_by, sort_dir, page, page_size). The CSV and SQLite versions share identical in-Python filtering logic for the non-database-native filters.
code_quality.rationale: Triplicated filtering logic means any bug fix or new filter field must be applied in three places. This has already caused divergence: HyperdriveRepository also has a 4th copy at line 266. A shared FilterParams dataclass and common validation layer would prevent drift.
code_quality.currentState: csv_repository.py:74-124 has Python in-memory filter_cases. sqlite_repository.py:343+ builds SQL WHERE clauses. supabase_repository.py:442+ builds PostgREST queries. hyperdrive_repository.py:266+ is a 4th copy. All four share the identical 12-parameter signature and identical parameter validation logic.
code_quality.proposedChange: 1) Create immi_case_downloader/filter_params.py with a FilterParams dataclass and validate() method. 2) Add a CaseRepositoryMixin with shared parameter validation. Each repo's filter_cases() delegates to its native query approach (SQL, PostgREST, in-memory) but shares the parameter validation and sort direction logic via the mixin.
code_quality.bestPractice: DRY, Repository Pattern with shared interface

plan.title: Create FilterParams dataclass in filter_params.py
plan.description: Define a dataclass with the 12 shared parameters, their defaults, and a validate() method that checks sort_by is in ALLOWED_SORT_FIELDS and sort_dir in ('asc', 'desc').

plan.title: Update CsvRepository.filter_cases to accept FilterParams
plan.description: Refactor csv_repository.py filter_cases to use FilterParams. Keep backward-compat **kwargs wrapper. Run existing tests.

plan.title: Update SqliteRepository and SupabaseRepository to use FilterParams
plan.description: Repeat for sqlite_repository.py and supabase_repository.py. Update hyperdrive_repository.py last. Verify all test_*_repository.py tests pass.

code_quality.title: _judge_profile_payload() is a 235-line god function — extract sub-computations
code_quality.description: _judge_profile_payload() at api.py line 836 is 235 lines long. It initialises 14 Counter objects, runs a single mega-loop over all judge cases accumulating every possible metric (wins, visa breakdown, concept effectiveness, representation, country, court comparison), then assembles a large dict. It violates the 50-line function guideline and is the single most complex function in the codebase.
code_quality.rationale: A 235-line function with 14 mutable counter variables is extremely hard to unit-test, debug, and extend. Each sub-computation (visa breakdown, concept effectiveness, yearly trend, representation analysis) is independently testable as a pure function.
code_quality.currentState: Single function lines 836-1070 with: 14 Counter initialisations, 1 mega accumulation loop covering 8 different metrics simultaneously, and a large dict assembly block. Called by analytics_judge_leaderboard, analytics_judge_profile, and analytics_judge_compare.
code_quality.proposedChange:
Extract into composable sub-functions:
  _compute_win_stats(cases) -> WinStats
  _compute_visa_breakdown(cases, wins_by_case) -> list[dict]
  _compute_concept_effectiveness(cases, wins_by_case) -> list[dict]
  _compute_yearly_trend(cases, wins_by_case) -> list[dict]
  _compute_representation_analysis(cases, wins_by_case) -> dict
  _compute_country_breakdown(cases, wins_by_case) -> list[dict]
Each takes the judge_cases list and a pre-computed wins_by_case set. _judge_profile_payload() becomes an orchestrator calling these sub-functions.

plan.title: Extract _compute_win_stats and pre-compute wins_by_case set
plan.description: Create _compute_win_stats(cases) -> dict that returns total, wins, losses, overall_rate, court_type. Pre-compute a set of case_ids that are wins to avoid re-calling _is_win in every sub-function.

plan.title: Extract the 6 sub-computation functions
plan.description: Extract _compute_visa_breakdown, _compute_concept_effectiveness, _compute_yearly_trend, _compute_nature_breakdown, _compute_representation_analysis, _compute_country_breakdown. Each receives cases list and win_set.

plan.title: Refactor _judge_profile_payload to orchestrator
plan.description: Replace the 235-line body with calls to the extracted functions. Confirm test_analytics_judge_api.py still passes.

Each section is a large JSX block with no sub-component extraction.

All rendered inline with no sub-components.

code_quality.title: 39 broad `except Exception` catches in api.py swallow errors silently
code_quality.description: api.py contains 39 `except Exception` clauses. Of these, many (lines 323, 357, 1312, 1676, 1805, 1836, 2003, 2035, 2066, 2115, 2175, 2435, 2505, 3103, 3193) log nothing or only log at WARNING without the exception details, making production debugging very difficult. Several are marked 'pragma: no cover' indicating they are never tested.
code_quality.rationale: Broad catches without logging the exception type/traceback turn transient bugs into silent failures. The Flask error model means unhandled exceptions become 500s with full traceback — which is often more useful than a swallowed exception returning a degraded result.
code_quality.currentState:
39 `except Exception` clauses. Many examples:
- Line 323: `except Exception: pass` (completely silent)
- Line 1312: `except Exception: return None` (silent)
- Line 2003/2035/2066: cache-fill fallbacks that swallow all errors
- Lines 3373/3433/3486/3524: RPC fallbacks only log at WARNING without exc_info=True
code_quality.proposedChange: 1) For every `except Exception: pass` or `except Exception: return X` with no log — add `logger.debug(..., exc_info=True)` at minimum. 2) For RPC fallbacks that are expected failures — keep WARNING but add `exc_info=True` so the traceback is captured at DEBUG level. 3) For cache-fill exceptions — re-raise after logging or use `logger.exception()`. Audit all 39 instances against this policy.
code_quality.bestPractice: Error handling: log detailed error context on server side (coding-style.md), never silently swallow errors

plan.title: Audit all 39 except Exception sites and categorise
plan.description: Create a list of: (a) completely silent catches, (b) catches that log without exc_info, (c) catches that correctly use logger.exception or exc_info=True. Target: every site in category (a) or (b) gets fixed.

plan.title: Add exc_info=True to all RPC fallback logger.warning calls
plan.description: The 4 RPC fallback catches in analytics endpoints (lines 3373, 3433, 3486, 3524) should all use logger.warning(..., exc_info=True) so the underlying cause is traceable without changing behavior.

plan.title: Replace silent catches with at minimum logger.debug
plan.description: For completely silent `except Exception: pass` patterns, add `logger.debug('Context description', exc_info=True)`. For cache-miss patterns, use `logger.exception()` to always capture full traceback.

LlmCouncilPage.tsx becomes ~200 lines of state + layout.

code_quality.title: 25% of Python functions across the project are missing return type hints
code_quality.description: Across 487 Python functions in the project, 125 (25%) have no return type annotation. In api.py specifically, 51 public route handler functions have no return type. The project uses Python 3.14 with full type-annotation support available.
code_quality.rationale: Missing return type hints prevent pyright/mypy from catching return-value misuse. Flask route handlers should all be annotated as `-> Response` or `-> tuple[Response, int]` to catch the common bug of accidentally returning a plain dict instead of jsonify(dict).
code_quality.currentState: 125 of 487 Python functions (25%) lack return type hints. All 51 public route handlers in api.py are missing return annotations (get_csrf_token, stats, list_cases, analytics_outcomes, etc.). Private helpers like _is_win, _round_rate, _split_judges do have type hints — the problem is concentrated in public-facing functions.
code_quality.proposedChange: Add return type annotations to all 51 route handlers using `from flask import Response`: route handlers return `Response`, handlers that return error tuples return `tuple[Response, int]`. Enable pyright in strict mode for the web/routes/ directory via a per-directory pyrightconfig. Fix all type errors surfaced.
code_quality.bestPractice: Type safety, Python 3.14 type annotation best practices

plan.title: Add return type hints to all 51 public route handlers in api.py
plan.description: Each Flask route handler returns either Response (via jsonify/_analytics_response/send_file) or tuple[Response, int] for error cases. Add these annotations and run python3 -m pyright immi_case_downloader/web/routes/api.py to surface any type errors.

plan.title: Add return type hints to helpers.py, jobs.py, web/__init__.py
plan.description: Apply the same annotation pass to the remaining files with missing hints. Focus on public functions first.

code_quality.title: frontend/src/lib/api.ts at 1291 lines — split typed API client by domain
code_quality.description: api.ts is 1291 lines containing all API call functions, TypeScript response types, and the CSRF-aware fetch wrapper for every domain: cases, analytics, search, taxonomy, LLM council, legislations, export, jobs. It is imported by nearly every hook file.
code_quality.rationale: A 1291-line single file for all API types and calls creates wide import surface area. Any change to one domain type causes the entire file to be re-evaluated by TypeScript. Splitting by domain enables tree-shaking, improves build-time type checking, and makes it clear which types belong to which feature.
code_quality.currentState: 1291 lines with 79 exports. All API call functions and their return types live in a single file. The file has been incrementally extended for every new feature (legislations, LLM council, taxonomy, semantic search) without a split strategy.
code_quality.proposedChange:
Split into domain-scoped modules under frontend/src/lib/api/:
  core.ts — fetchWithCsrf, ApiError, shared types
  cases.ts — CaseFilters, ImmigrationCase, fetchCases, fetchCase, ...
  analytics.ts — AnalyticsOutcomes, fetchAnalyticsOutcomes, ...
  taxonomy.ts — VisaLookup, fetchTaxonomyJudges, ...
  search.ts — SearchResult, fetchSearch, fetchSemanticSearch
  llm-council.ts — LlmCouncilResponse, fetchLlmCouncil, ...
  legislations.ts — Legislation, fetchLegislations, ...
frontend/src/lib/api.ts becomes a re-export barrel.
code_quality.bestPractice: File size ≤800 lines, high cohesion modules

code_quality.title: ESLint config missing no-console and explicit-function-return-type rules
code_quality.description: The project's eslint.config.ts uses typescript-eslint recommended preset but does not add @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type or no-console rules. The tsconfig.app.json is appropriately strict (strict: true, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters) but the ESLint layer has no runtime-behavior rules beyond react-hooks.
code_quality.rationale: no-console prevents debug console.log statements leaking to production. explicit-function-return-type enforces type-safe function signatures in React components and hooks. Both are low-friction rules with high signal value for a codebase this size.
code_quality.currentState: eslint.config.ts uses: js.configs.recommended + tseslint.configs.recommended + reactHooks.configs.flat.recommended + reactRefresh.configs.vite. No custom rules object. TypeScript strict mode is enabled in tsconfig but ESLint does not enforce function return types or ban console usage.
code_quality.proposedChange:
Add a rules block to eslint.config.ts:
  'no-console': ['warn', { allow: ['warn', 'error'] }]
  '@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type': ['warn', { allowExpressions: true, allowTypedFunctionExpressions: true }]
The allowExpressions option means inline arrow functions in JSX props are exempt — only module-level functions and React components require explicit returns.
code_quality.bestPractice: Linting as first defence against debug code leaks and missing types

plan.title: Add no-console and explicit-function-return-type rules to eslint.config.ts
plan.description: Add rules block with the two rules. Run `cd frontend && npx eslint src/ --max-warnings 0` to see the current violation count before deciding whether to enable as 'error' or 'warn'.

plan.title: Fix or suppress pre-existing violations
plan.description: For any genuine console.log statements that should remain (e.g., error boundaries), replace with console.warn/error or add eslint-disable comments with justification. For explicit-function-return-type violations, add return types to the flagged components.
